Sovereign question router · v62

Ask once.
Receive the proof path.

Ask GoalOS answers from the canonical route manifest, local doctrine cards, and public-safe page descriptions. It explains the best route, shows alternatives, and can navigate only when the visitor explicitly asks it to open a page.

Open the sovereign question console →Launch Mission AutopilotSee all 67 routesRead the proof institution

No account. No form submission. No wallet. No token approval. No network request. No analytics. No cookies. No browser storage. No user data retained. No production authority.

AskGoalOS
QuestionIntentRouteReceipt
67public routes
0network calls
0stored questions
1route engine

Live local answer engine · no server

Type a question. GoalOS explains the route.

Matched intentAwaiting question
Route confidenceLocal score

Best-practice boundary

Answers are local. Navigation is deliberate.

The router scores the question against intent cards and the canonical route manifest. It gives a short answer, a best next page, alternate routes, confidence, and a downloadable receipt. It opens a page only when the user asks for navigation.

Proof institution fit

Questions become local receipts, not user records.

The page demonstrates the same principle as GoalOS itself: make intent legible, route to evidence, expose boundaries, and avoid collecting private data when public guidance is enough.

GoalOS Care Command

Start with one objective.

Tell GoalOS what you want. It prepares the proof path, answers questions locally, and routes you to the right page while every preserved page remains available.

Open Care Command →All pagesAsk GoalOS
